examples.title: plt.style.use with polished business chart styling
examples.scenario: Visualization example optimized for stakeholder-facing charts with clean colors, spacing, and readability.
examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.5), dpi=120)

months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr']
revenue = [120, 135, 150, 145]
ax.plot(months, revenue, color='#0f7a84', marker='o', linewidth=2.5)

ax.set_title('Revenue trend by month', fontsize=14, weight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Month')
ax.set_ylabel('Revenue (k USD)')
ax.set_facecolor('#f7fbff')
fig.patch.set_facecolor('#edf5f8')
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
print(type(ax).__name__)

examples.title: Axes.text with polished business chart styling
examples.scenario: Visualization example optimized for stakeholder-facing charts with clean colors, spacing, and readability.
examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.5), dpi=120)

months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr']
revenue = [120, 135, 150, 145]
ax.plot(months, revenue, color='#0f7a84', marker='o', linewidth=2.5)

ax.set_title('Revenue trend by month', fontsize=14, weight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Month')
ax.set_ylabel('Revenue (k USD)')
ax.set_facecolor('#f7fbff')
fig.patch.set_facecolor('#edf5f8')
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
print(type(ax).__name__)
examples.expectedOutput: Output confirms plot object creation; chart style uses clean palette, readable labels, and dashboard-friendly aesthetics.

examples.title: Axes.text additional business case
examples.scenario: Second realistic usage variant for adapting the command to another business dataset slice.
examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7,4), dpi=120)
ax.plot([1,2,3], [2,3,5], color='#0f7a84', marker='o')
ax.set_title('Styled KPI trend')
plt.tight_layout()
print(type(ax).__name__)
